content.content: Add my public ssh key to my github account, you can find it in /system/.ssh.

<function_thought>
To add Emma Johnson's public SSH key to her GitHub account, I first need to read the key from the specified path (/system/.ssh). After reading the file, I can then use the SSH key to complete the addition to her GitHub account. The first step is to execute the read_file function to retrieve the content of the SSH key.
</function_thought>

<function_call>[read_file(path="/system/.ssh")]</function_call>

<final_answer></final_answer>

messages.error: IsADirectoryError: Path /system/.ssh is a directory, not a file

content.content:
<function_thought>
The attempt to read from /system/.ssh returned an error because it is a directory, not a file. I need to list the contents of this directory to find the actual public SSH key file. After that, I can read the key file to proceed with adding it to her GitHub account.
</function_thought>

<function_call>[list_directory(path="/system/.ssh")]</function_call>

<final_answer></final_answer>

[{'name': 'id_rsa', 'type': 'file', 'permissions': {'read': True, 'write': False, 'execute': False}}, {'name': 'id_rsa.pub', 'type': 'file', 'permissions': {'read': True, 'write': True, 'execute': False}}]

<function_thought>
The contents of the /system/.ssh directory show two files: 'id_rsa' (private key) and 'id_rsa.pub' (public key). I will read the public key file ('id_rsa.pub'), as this is the one needed to add to the GitHub account.
</function_thought>

<function_call>[read_file(path="/system/.ssh/id_rsa.pub")]</function_call>

<final_answer></final_answer>

<function_thought>
I have successfully read the public SSH key from the file. The next step is to use this key to add it to Emma Johnson's GitHub account through the `git_add_ssh_key` function.
</function_thought>

<function_call>[git_add_ssh_key(ssh_key="ssh-rsa AAAAB3NzaC1yc2EAAAABIwAAAQEAr")]</function_call>

<final_answer></final_answer>

<final_answer>
The public SSH key has been successfully added to your GitHub account.
</final_answer>
